title: Good Manufacturing Practices - Request for Inspection of a Foreign Site Form (FRM-0213) - Canada.ca
content:
## Good Manufacturing Practices - Request for Inspection of a Foreign Site Form (FRM-0213)
This HTML document is not a form. Its purpose is to display the information as found on the form for viewing purposes only. If you wish to use the form, you must use the alternate format below.
(Word Version - 96 K)
(PDF Version - 34 K)
Section 1: Requester Information
- Drug Establishment Licence Number:
Section 2: Foreign Building Information
- Foreign Building Name:
Section 3: Drug Information
Product Name and/or DIN:
- Microbiological – Sterility
- = Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient
- = Blood & Blood Components
- = Radiopharmaceutical
- = Powder for solution
- = Bulk Intermediates (biological only)
Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API)
If you indicated non-sterile API, is the API being used to manufacture a sterile or non-sterile Drug in Dosage Form? ( Yes No)
Section 4: Status of Foreign Building
- Is the building listed on your DEL or Table A? (Yes No)
- Are you adding the building in support of a Drug Submission? (Yes No) If yes, when are you planning to submit the drug application?
- If yes, when are you planning to submit the drug application?
- Have you contacted the foreign building to notify them of the request you have submitted for an on-site assessment by Health Canada? (Yes No) If yes, have they proposed dates? (Yes No) If yes again, what are the proposed dates?
- If yes, have they proposed dates? (Yes No)
- If yes again, what are the proposed dates?
- Has the building fabricated, packaged/labelled or tested drugs destined for the Canadian market in the past 4 years? (Yes No) If yes, since when? If no, when do they expect to start activities for drugs destined for the Canadian market?
- If no, when do they expect to start activities for drugs destined for the Canadian market?
- To the best of your knowledge, is there a planned inspection by a Qualified Footnote 1 Regulatory Footnote 2 Authority at this time? (Yes No) If yes, by whom and when?
- If yes, by whom and when?
- To the best of your knowledge, has there been a recent inspection by a Qualified Footnote 1 Regulatory Authority Footnote 2 ? (Yes No) If yes, by whom and when?
- If yes, by whom and when?
Section 5: Rationale for Request
Section 6: Available Documentation
Is a Site Master/Reference file available? (Yes No)
* Do not submit the SMF/SRF with this request. It is to be provided upon request
Section 7: Authorization
- Name of Authorized Signing Official:
Qualified authority - An authority listed as a member of the Pharmaceutical Inspection Cooperation Scheme (PIC/S)
Return to footnote 1 referrer
Regulatory authority - A government agency or other entity in an MRA country that has a legal right to control the use or sale of drugs within that country and that may take enforcement action to ensure that drugs marketed within its jurisdiction comply with legal requirements.
Return to footnote 2 referrer
